data.title: The Hyperliquid team address has begun transferring tokens out in batches, with the largest single transaction involving approximately 75,000 HYPE tokens.
data.description: According to on-chain transaction records, the address marked "HyperLabs," after unstaking and conducting a small-scale test at noon today, began executing a larger-scale fund transfer between 14:26 and 14:49. Data shows that the address transferred tokens to multiple different wallets in batches, primarily between 9,000 and 75,000 HYPE, with the largest single transfer being approximately 74,999 HYPE. Transfers are still ongoing. Previously, the Hyperliquid team wallet had redeemed 2.6 million HYPE (approximately $90.18 million) to its spot account.

data.title: The People's Bank of China held a meeting to crack down on speculation in virtual currency trading, emphasizing the risks of stablecoins.
data.description: The People's Bank of China convened a meeting of its coordination mechanism for combating speculation in virtual currencies on November 28. Officials from the Ministry of Public Security, the Cyberspace Administration of China, the Supreme People's Court, and the Supreme People's Procuratorate attended the meeting. The meeting noted a recent resurgence of speculation in virtual currencies, with related illegal and criminal activities occurring frequently. The meeting reiterated that virtual currencies lack legal tender status and specifically pointed out that stablecoins, as a form of virtual currency, currently cannot effectively meet customer identification and anti-money laundering requirements, posing risks such as being used for money laundering and illegal cross-border fund transfers. All departments will continue to adhere to the "prohibitory policy" towards virtual currencies, focusing on information and capital flows, and will continue to severely crack down on related illegal financial activities.

data.title: How a Single Translation Helped Spark China’s First Wave of Bitcoin Adoption
data.description:

data.title: Binance Alpha: Users with at least 256 points can claim a 400 GAIX airdrop.
data.description: According to official news, trading of GaiAi (GAIX) on Binance Alpha will begin on November 29, 2025 at 16:00 (UTC+8). Users holding at least 256 Binance Alpha points can claim a token airdrop. 400 GAIX tokens can be claimed on the Alpha event page. If the event continues, the point threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every five minutes. Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Alpha points. Users must confirm their claim within 24 hours on the Alpha event page; otherwise, they will be considered to have forfeited their airdrop.

data.title: The Hyperliquid team wallet unstaking 2.6 million HYPE tokens and conducted small-scale sell-offs and transfer tests.
data.description: According to on-chain data monitoring, the Hyperliquid team wallet (0x43...a251) redeemed 2.6 million HYPE (approximately $90.18 million) from its staked position to its spot account at 12:32 today. The latest transaction records show that this address subsequently executed a spot sale of 1 HYPE and multiple transfers of 1 HYPE to different addresses, suggesting that it may be testing related functionality. Currently, this wallet still holds approximately 240 million staked HYPE.

data.title: Cango's Bitcoin Holdings Surpass 6,900 BTC
data.description: According to Odaily, Bitcoin mining company Cango, listed on the New York Stock Exchange, announced on the X platform that it produced 129.1 BTC this week. The company's total Bitcoin holdings have now exceeded 6,900 BTC, reaching 6,902.5 BTC.

data.title: Community Donates to Animoca Hong Kong Relief Fund via Flap Platform
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the community has contributed to the Animoca Hong Kong Relief Fund through the Flap platform's tax token mechanism. The transaction fee income from this token is directed to the relief fund's EVM address. Currently, 77 BNB have been donated to the fund via the Flap platform.

The Animoca Hong Kong Relief Fund is a nonprofit charitable initiative launched by Animoca, inviting the crypto community to aid in the relief efforts for the Hong Kong fire incident. Animoca has pledged to convert the received donations into Hong Kong dollars and donate 100% of the proceeds to the Hong Kong Red Cross to support rescue and recovery operations.

data.title: CoinShares Withdraws SEC Application for Staked Solana ETF
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, asset manager CoinShares has withdrawn its application with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for a staked Solana exchange-traded fund (ETF). The SEC filing revealed that the structuring deal and asset purchase for the proposed fund were not completed. The filing stated, "The Registration Statement sought to register shares to be issued in connection with a transaction that was ultimately not effectuated. No shares were sold, or will be sold, pursuant to the above-mentioned Registration Statement."

The first staked Solana (SOL) ETF in the United States was issued by REX-Osprey in June, followed by Bitwise's staked SOL ETF in October. Bitwise's ETF launched with nearly $223 million in assets on its first trading day, achieving about half the value of the REX-Osprey ETF, which had been trading for several months. Despite the launch of these ETFs and investor interest, the price of SOL has been on a downward trend since reaching over $250 per coin in September.

Solana ETFs attracted over $369 million in capital flows during November, as investors sought the yield-bearing opportunities of staked SOL investment vehicles offering 5-7% staking rewards. This trend contrasted with Bitcoin and Ether ETFs, which experienced record outflows during October and November. Analysts had previously forecasted SOL reaching as high as $400 due to capital inflows from ETFs. However, price projections have been revised downward since October, with some analysts now suggesting that SOL faces challenges in reclaiming $150.

SOL's price hit a five-month low of approximately $120 in November, representing a 60% decrease from its all-time high of around $295 reached in January 2025. The token's significant rise in January was attributed to the launch of the Official Trump memecoin on the Solana network, which fueled memecoin trading on the platform.

data.description: Binance founder CZ posted on the X platform that it is better to sell when the market is extremely greedy and buy when it is extremely fearful, although this may be an unpopular opinion.

data.title: Tianfeng Securities Faces Investigation by Regulatory Authorities
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Tianfeng Securities has announced that the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) has issued a 'Notice of Investigation' due to alleged violations related to information disclosure and illegal financing. The CSRC has decided to investigate Tianfeng Securities based on the Securities Law and Administrative Penalty Law.

A non-bank analyst suggests that if Tianfeng Securities is ultimately subjected to administrative penalties, it could negatively impact the company's business operations and financing, potentially leading to liquidity challenges. Previously, Tianfeng Securities disclosed that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Tianfeng International Securities and Futures Limited, had received approval from the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission to obtain a third-class license related to virtual assets, allowing it to offer virtual asset trading services through comprehensive account arrangements.

data.title: Kalshi faces new class action lawsuit over sports betting claims
data.description:
A group of customers has filed a lawsuit against Kalshi Inc. , claiming the prediction market illegally operates as a sports bookmaker and deceives users about how it conducts business. Seven users brought the case to a New York court on Wednesday. They say the company tricks customers by calling itself a provider of “legal sports […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/kalshi-new-class-action-lawsuit/

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $71.371 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $71.371 million yesterday (November 28th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was Ark Invest and 21Shares' ETF ARKB, with a net inflow of $88.044 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.828 billion. This was followed by Fidelity's ETF FBTC, with a net inflow of $77.4532 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $12.029 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock's ETF IBIT, with a net outflow of $114 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $62.566 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $119.391 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.56%, and a cumulative net inflow of $57.705 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $76.5491 million yesterday, marking the fifth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $76.5491 million yesterday (November 28th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (ETHA) saw the largest single-day net inflow of $68.2659 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $13.147 billion. The Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHE) followed with a net inflow of $8.2832 million, while its historical total net outflow is $4.932 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $19.155 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 5.19%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $12.945 billion.
